text:
Market today: Mixed. Chip demand remains strong, but rising oil prices and geopolitical tensions (China trade) create macro headwinds. These can impact growth-oriented tech names.

Your holdings: SOXL (-36.74%) and SPAL (-55.27%) are leveraged and deeply in the red, flagged "SELL/NEW." Their listed stops are not functioning as stop-losses. Margin amplifies these drops. QQQ (-1.3%) and QQQI (-3.67%) are also flagged "TRIM" with small losses.

Watch: Closely monitor your leveraged SOXL and SPAL positions for further downside. Keep an eye on the chip sector's resilience given the positive news on Nvidia and SK Hynix.

Bottom line: Prioritize managing risk in your highly concentrated, leveraged book, especially with macro headwinds emerging.
plays: Re-evaluate SOXL and SPAL. Both are leveraged, deeply negative, and flagged "SELL/NEW." Their listed stops are ineffective. Consider cutting losses to protect margin.
Trim QQQ or QQQI. Both are flagged "TRIM" with small losses. Reduce tech concentration or free up capital as a defensive move.
Consider NVDU long: Scanner shows a "daily discount" setup. Entry 131.71-131.58, stop 131.06, backed by Nvidia chip demand news.
